avoid: Skip if you want vanilla ToB ending
tip: The community standard for ToB. Install Balthazar redemption for a non-evil path.

avoid: Can bloat BG1 with too much dialog if combined with every dialog mod
tip: The definitive BG1 dialog expansion. Most BG1 NPC mods are designed to work alongside it.
Install on Core/Hard first. Insane SCS is a very different game. Use Enhanced Powergaming Scripts for auto-buffing to match.

avoid: Proficiency changes may clash with ToF
tip: The most compatible tweak mod. Install most components but be selective with proficiency/class restriction changes.
Modern UI but still in development. Set Force_Difficulty_Widget=1 in stratagems.ini for SCS compatibility.
Foundation mod for advanced features. Most UI and script mods benefit from having EEex installed first.
Keeps Imoen available through SoD and early SoA. Great for story continuity in EET.

avoid: Too many sorcerer kits can be overwhelming
Multiclass specialists + SCS can cause edge cases
tip: Pick a focused subset. Revised Metamagic and Spell Switching are the most impactful components.

mod: Dark Side of the Sword Coast
goes_with: Northern Tales of the Sword Coast
avoid: Can make BG1 very long if combined with all quest mods
tip: Classic BG1 mega-expansion. The EET version is different from the BG:EE standalone version.
Lets you explore after the final fight. Korlasz Dungeon in BG1 component is great for EET continuity.
